content: We can frame Self-Refine, the task-specific iterative self-improvement framework for LLMs, as a single-perspective foundational 'seed' that can be extended by integrating multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to create a multi-perspective iterative self-refinement framework. This hybrid framework would retain Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra supervised training or reinforcement learning, while resolving the common failure mode of single-perspective self-refinement getting stuck in local optima of incorrect output.
